title: Nageia neriifolia
taxonomy: Nageia neriifolia is a species of coniferous tree in the cypress family Cupressaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and belongs to the genus Nageia, which is part of the family Cupressaceae. The classification of Nageia neriifolia is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Gymnosperms, Class: Pinopsida, Order: Cupressales, Family: Cupressaceae, Genus: Nageia, Species: N. neriifolia. This classification is based on the tree's morphology and molecular characteristics. Nageia neriifolia is also known as the "Nageia neriifolia tree" or "Nageia neriifolia conifer".
uses: Nageia neriifolia, also known as the Nageia or the Nageia neriifolia tree, is a species of coniferous tree native to Southeast Asia. The leaves and bark of the Nageia neriifolia tree have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In traditional medicine, the bark is used to treat fever, while the leaves are used to treat various health conditions, including skin problems and respiratory issues. The leaves also have antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infection. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses of Nageia neriifolia.
benefits: Nageia neriifolia, also known as the Nageia or Nageia neriifolia, has several reported health benefits.
